dal.name: Dal Tadka
steps: Wash 1 cup toor dal and soak for 15 mins.
Add dal, 2.5 cups water, salt, turmeric, and 1 tomato (chopped) to the Hawkins cooker.
Close lid and pressure cook on high flame for 3 whistles.
Turn off flame. Wait for pressure to release naturally (10 mins).
Open and mash the dal lightly.
Heat 2 tbsp ghee in a pan. Add cumin seeds, dried red chilli, garlic, and hing.
Add onion and cook till golden. Pour tadka over dal.
Garnish with coriander and serve hot with rice or roti.
dal.tips: Add a pinch of asafoetida (hing) to the tadka for authentic flavour. For creamier dal, reduce water to 2 cups.

rice.name: Steamed Rice
steps: Wash 1 cup rice under cold water until water runs clear.
Add rice and 1.5 cups water to the Hawkins cooker.
Add a pinch of salt and ½ tsp oil (prevents sticking).
Close lid. Cook on high flame until 2 whistles.
Lower flame to minimum for 5 mins.
Turn off and wait for pressure to release (10 mins).
Fluff rice gently with a fork before serving.
rice.tips: The 1:1.5 rice-to-water ratio works perfectly in Hawkins cookers. Soaking rice for 20 mins reduces cooking time.

biryani.name: Chicken Biryani
steps: Marinate 500g chicken with 1 cup yogurt, spices (biryani masala, garam masala), salt, and lemon juice for 30 mins.
Wash 2 cups basmati rice and soak for 20 mins.
Heat 3 tbsp oil in Hawkins cooker. Fry sliced onions till golden brown. Remove half for garnish.
Add whole spices (bay leaf, cardamom, cloves, cinnamon) and cook for 1 min.
Add marinated chicken and cook for 10 mins, stirring occasionally.
Drain soaked rice and add to cooker. Add 2.5 cups warm water.
Add saffron milk, fried onions, mint leaves.
Close lid and give 2 whistles on high flame, then 5 mins on low.
Let pressure release naturally. Open and mix gently.
Serve with raita.
biryani.tips: Do not over-stir after opening — the layered look makes biryani special! Use aged basmati for best results.

khichdi.name: Khichdi
steps: Wash ½ cup rice and ½ cup moong dal together.
Heat 1 tbsp ghee in Hawkins cooker. Add cumin seeds and asafoetida.
Add 1 onion, 1 tomato, green chilli, ginger — sauté for 3 mins.
Add rice-dal mixture, turmeric, salt, and 3 cups water.
Close lid. Cook for 3-4 whistles on medium flame.
Let pressure release naturally.
Open and mix. Adjust consistency with warm water.
Top with a generous spoon of ghee before serving.
khichdi.tips: For a smoother khichdi, add 4 cups water. Great with pickle and papad!

rajma.name: Rajma Masala
steps: Soak 1 cup rajma overnight (8-10 hours).
Pressure cook soaked rajma with 3 cups water and salt for 5 whistles.
Let pressure release naturally. Check that rajma is soft.
Heat oil in a pan. Add onion paste and cook till golden (8 mins).
Add ginger-garlic paste, tomato puree, and all spices. Cook till oil separates.
Add cooked rajma with its water to the masala.
Simmer for 10 mins till gravy thickens.
Garnish with butter and fresh coriander. Serve with steamed rice.
rajma.tips: Don't skip overnight soaking — it drastically reduces cooking time and makes rajma easier to digest.

pulao.name: Vegetable Pulao
steps: Wash and soak 1 cup basmati rice for 20 mins.
Heat 2 tbsp ghee in Hawkins cooker. Add whole spices and bay leaf.
Add sliced onion and cook till translucent.
Add mixed vegetables (carrot, peas, beans, potato), salt, and spices.
Drain rice and add. Mix gently for 2 mins.
Add 1.5 cups water and check seasoning.
Close lid. Give 2 whistles on high, then 3 mins on low flame.
Let pressure release. Fluff with fork and serve hot.
pulao.tips: Adding a tablespoon of lemon juice brightens the flavours beautifully.

halwa.name: Suji Halwa
steps: Heat 3 tbsp ghee in Hawkins pressure cooker (open, without lid).
Add 1 cup semolina (suji) and roast on low flame, stirring continuously for 5-7 mins till golden.
Add 2 cups warm water carefully (it will splutter) and mix quickly.
Add ¾ cup sugar and cardamom powder. Mix well.
Close lid. Give 1 whistle on low flame. Turn off.
Let pressure release. Open and stir vigorously.
Garnish with cashews fried in ghee and raisins.
Serve warm.
halwa.tips: Roasting suji properly is the secret — it should smell nutty before you add water.

soup.name: Tomato Soup
steps: Add 4 tomatoes (quartered), 1 onion, 3 garlic cloves, ½ carrot, and 1 cup water to Hawkins cooker.
Close lid. Give 3 whistles on high flame.
Let pressure release. Cool slightly.
Blend the cooked mixture to a smooth puree.
Strain through a sieve for a silky smooth soup.
Return to pot. Add salt, pepper, sugar, and cream.
Simmer for 5 mins. Adjust consistency.
Serve hot with croutons and a drizzle of cream.
soup.tips: Add a pinch of sugar to balance the acidity of tomatoes.
